{{Quote|The princess of the Mudgub tribe!|Gorph guard, announcing another candidate for Prince Vork's hand in marriage|<ref name="PL" />}}
The '''Mudgubs''' were a [[Species/Legends|species]] of [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] bipeds who lived on the [[Endor/Legends|Forest Moon of Endor]]. They had large [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] and long, blunt snouts, as well as hairy, [[Ear/Legends|ear]]-like projections on the sides of the head. Rather than standing straight up like [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] species, Mudgubs squatted with their [[Limb/Legends|legs]] splayed. The Mudgubs of Endor were organized as a tribe and recognized at least some of their members as [[King/Legends|royalty]].

In [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], the [[Mudgub princess]] was invited to the [[Gorphs' grotto|grotto home]] of a pompous species known as the [[Gorph/Legends|Gorphs]]. There, she had an audience with [[Queen/Legends|Queen]] [[Slugga]] for consideration as a bride to [[Prince/Legends|Prince]] [[Vork (Gorph)|Vork]]. Eventually, however, the queen rejected the princess.

==Biology and appearance==
{{Quote|I like her, Ma! I like her!|Prince Vork, upon seeing the princess of the Mudgub tribe|<ref name="PL" />}}
The Mudgubs were a [[Species/Legends|species]] of [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]], [[Color/Legends|gray]]-skinned bipeds<ref name="PL" /> who stood about 0.33 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] tall.<ref name="height" /> They had long, thin [[Limb/Legends|legs]] that, when at rest, splayed out to either side and folded at the knees so as to bring the belly close to the ground. Their feet were narrow and long, with three pointed toes. Their arms, also thin, were short in comparison to the rest of the body and terminated in hands with three fingers each. A long, wispy tail grew from the Mudgub posterior and ended in a barbed point.<ref name="PL" />

The Mudgub head was large in relation to the body and was wider than it was tall. A bulbous snout was the dominant feature; this was divided by a wide [[Mouth/Legends|mouth]] with plastic lips that could show emotion. Whether naturally or via make-up, some Mudgubs had large, red lips. Above the snout sat two bulbous white [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] with long eyelashes. Toward the back of the head were two projections that resembled [[Ear/Legends|ears]] with dark-colored fuzz fringing them. Some Mudgubs wore jewelry, such as [[Piercing/Legends|earrings]] and [[Necklace/Legends|necklaces]].<ref name="PL" />

==Society and culture==
Mudgubs lived in tribes, and at least one member of the species was recognized as a [[Prince/Legends|princess]]; her status was indicated by a [[Gold/Legends|gold]]-colored [[Crown/Legends|crown]]. Some Mudgubs had high-pitched voices. The Mudgubs of the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] [[Endor/Legends|Endor]] had relations with other groups, including the [[Gorph/Legends|Gorphs]] led by [[Queen/Legends|Queen]] [[Slugga]].<ref name="PL" />

==History==
{{Quote|Eh! Much too common!|Queen Slugga, just prior to casting the princess of the Mudgubs into the pit|<ref name="PL" />}}
[[File:Mudgub_princess_cast_out.png|thumb|right|220px|The Mudgub princess was deemed too common for Prince Vork and cast out of the Gorphs' grotto.]]
In [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]],<ref name="EwoksDate">{{EwoksDate|appearance of the Mudgubs|[[Princess Latara]]}}</ref> the [[Mudgub princess|princess of the Mudgub tribe]] of Endor was chosen for an audience with Queen Slugga of the Gorphs as a potential bride for her son, Prince [[Vork (Gorph)|Vork]]. The princess was brought to an antechamber in the [[Gorphs' grotto]], where she awaited her summons along with the [[Titterbug princess|princess]] of the [[Titterbug]]s. The Mudgub princess was escorted before the queen first. Although Vork seemed to approve of her, Queen Slugga felt her too common and ordered her thrown into a large hole in the grotto floor known as "[[Pit (Gorph's grotto)|the pit]]." The Mudgub princess fell through with a shriek.<ref name="PL" />

==Behind the scenes==
Mudgub appeared in "[[Princess Latara]],"<ref name="PL" /> an episode of the [[Ewoks (television series)|''Ewoks'']] animated series that first aired on [[September 20]], [[1986]]. It was written by [[Paul Dini]].<ref name="Insider">{{InsiderCite|27|A Star Wars CELibration|A ''Star Wars'' CELibration}}</ref> The story features only one Mudgub character, who has no lines. Her scream is provided by an uncredited voice artist.

==Appearances==
*{{Ewoks|Princess Latara}} {{1st}}
